opening_hours: Outdoor streets accessible 24/7; museums typically 09:00–16:30 (closed Mon)
entry_fee: Free to explore streets; Songkhla National Museum 150 THB foreigners, 30 THB Thais
best_time_to_visit: Early morning or late afternoon for cooler walking weather and better light for street art photos

description.en:
## A Living Museum of Southern Thai Heritage

Songkhla Old Town is a beautifully preserved historic quarter about 30 kilometres east of Hat Yai, sitting on a narrow peninsula between the Gulf of Thailand and Songkhla Lake. Its narrow lanes are lined with Sino-Portuguese shophouses, Chinese shrines, and colonial-era merchants' warehouses that date back to the early 19th century.

## Architecture and Street Art

The architectural highlight is the blend of Far Eastern and Western styles. Walking along Nakhon Nok, Nakhon Nai, and Nang Ngam Roads you will discover colourful Sino-Portuguese facades, ornate Chinese clan houses, and weathered wooden shopfronts. In recent years the neighbourhood has become famous for its vibrant street art — ceramic wall installations, large-scale murals depicting local fishing life, and quirky painted doorways transform the old walls into an open-air gallery.

## Museums and Cultural Sites

The Songkhla National Museum occupies a grand Sino-Portuguese building and houses artefacts tracing the region's maritime trading history. Nearby, the Hub Ho Hin Red Rice Mill is a former rice factory converted into a cultural exhibition space. The Nakhon Nai Museum, set in a colonial-style house, showcases everyday life in old Songkhla. The Old Town Gate marks the historic entrance to the once-walled city and is a popular photo stop.

## Cafes and Local Food

Numerous charming cafes have opened inside restored shophouses, offering excellent coffee and air-conditioned respite. Try Cafe Der See for lakeside views, or stop at a street-side noodle shop for a bowl of kanom jeen (rice noodles with spicy fish curry), a Songkhla speciality.

## Getting There

From Hat Yai, take a minivan from the city centre (40 THB, 40 minutes) or hire a private car for about 400 THB one way.
description.nl: Historische stad met Sino-Portugese architectuur musea en culturele locaties

tips: Rent a bicycle to cover more ground — several guesthouses and cafes in the old town offer rentals for 50–100 THB per day
Combine with a visit to Samila Beach (5 minutes away) to see the famous golden mermaid statue
Wear comfortable walking shoes as the streets are paved with old cobblestones
